instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertion on What Is an Endowment Plan’s Premium? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: endowment policy, endowment plan, endowment insurance
Body Content:
While building a safety net for your dependents is important, saving for future contingencies and life goal fulfilment is also necessary. Endowment insurance plans help you achieve just that. These insurance products offer the perfect balance between a life cover and savings benefits. If you die before the policy ends, the death benefit from the same will be given to your listed nominee. This will help keep them financially stable in your absence. However, if you happen to survive the policy term, you can use the guaranteed returns from such plans to fulfil all your life goals right from planning for a comfortable retirement or funding your child’s education.
Much like other life insurance plans, endowment policies come with a definite tenure during which you have to pay premiums for the policy. Premiums for endowment plans can be paid either annually, monthly, quarterly or as a lump-sum one-time payment. However, it is important to note that these premium payments are not uniform for all, but rather are contingent on several factors. When buying endowment policies, it is important to review these factors and the stated premium rates to ensure you get the best coverage against a relatively reasonable pocket pinch.
Factors Affecting Endowment Premium Value in Endowment Plans
The premium for your endowment policy will depend on a host of factors. Your insurance provider will take the following factors into account when quoting your premium amount:
Age
Like all other life insurance policies, endowment insurance also takes your age into account when determining the premium amount. While endowment plans generally have an extensive age eligibility window, the premiums you pay vary as per your age. Since health risks tend to increase with age, the quoted premium rates also increase with age. Thus, ideally, you should purchase an endowment plan early on in life to reap maximise benefits at a low premium rate.
Gender
Insurers use statistical evidence on gender to fix premium amounts. For instance, if you’re a woman, you will likely be able to purchase an endowment plan with lower premiums. Insurance companies base this decision on scientific studies that suggest that women live 5 years longer than men, which implies that women will likely hold the policy for a longer duration than men. Hence, they are given the bonus of enjoying lower premium rates than men.
Sum Assured
When buying an endowment plan, one of the first things that you have to do is pick a sum assured amount. This sum assured amount is the lump sum payment your beneficiaries stand to derive from the endowment policy, in the event of your untimely demise during the policy term. Generally, for a higher sum assured amount, you pay higher premiums. However, some plans offer you rebates for a high sum assured selection. To enjoy this additional benefit, you can opt for such plans.
Medical History
Your medical history gives the insurer an idea of how risky you are as an applicant. For instance, if you’ve been diagnosed with a critical illness like cancer or heart problems, your premium rates will be higher than someone who has a clean medical history. This rule also applies if you’re currently healthy but have previously suffered from a serious medical condition.
Tobacco Use
Tobacco usage can seriously affect your premium rates. Generally, non-smokers live a relatively healthier and disease-free life than smokers and tobacco users. Since smoking or tobacco usage is correlated to life-threatening diseases like cancer, endowment insurance providers ensure that the premium for such individuals – as risky applicants- are higher than non-smokers. Thus, when applying for an endowment policy, if you’ve used tobacco in the last 12 months, you will be classed as a tobacco user and your premiums will be on the higher end.
Wrapping It Up
While dual benefit endowment plans can help you tackle both insurance and saving requirements all at once, the premiums you pay for such plans have to be evaluated before purchasing such policies. While this premium calculation process will vary from one insurance provider to the next, the factors listed above generally play a key role in determining your monthly, quarterly, bi-annual, or annual premium payments.
To ensure that you opt for the most optimised endowment plan, you can compare policies online to see which one offers you the best premium rates. You can also use online premium calculators to get quotes for your policy by filling in the given fields like age, gender, sum assured, etc. This will help you find a policy that best meets your savings and coverage goals without going overboard with the budget.
input.compliance_comments:
[Harshita Rajawat]: endowment policy (5) – 0.6%endowment plan (8) – 1.0%endowment insurance (3) – 0.3%The Total Keyword Density is 1.9% which is ideal for 764 words count article.The placements of the target keywords are apt. (Context: SEO Insertion on What Is an Endowment Plan’s Premium? - Article Page)
[Harshita Rajawat]: Can we add a sentence regarding how to calculate the premium amount with anchor text “Investment Calculator” (Context: While dual benefit endowment plans can help you tackle both insurance and saving...)
